scope_summary: WAPC position statement setting requirements for the consistent consideration and assessment of renewable energy facilities (principally wind turbines and solar arrays) in Western Australia. It applies to the preparation and assessment of region and local planning schemes, planning strategies, structure plans, and subdivision and development applications state-wide. It supersedes Planning Bulletin 67 Guidelines for Wind Farm Development (2004) and addresses zoning permissibility ('A' use recommendation), environmental, visual/landscape, noise, aviation safety, bushfire, heritage and construction/decommissioning considerations.

key_numeric_standards.topic: wind turbine separation from noise-sensitive land uses
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: minimum recommended distance between noise-sensitive land uses and a wind turbine (reducible with local government approval on DWER advice)
new wind turbines within 1,500 m of an existing or new noise-sensitive premises (excluding caretaker dwellings) - acoustic study required
minimum recommended clearance to combustible vegetation around wind turbines and solar arrays in bushfire prone areas
wind turbines and solar arrays should where possible not be located in bushfire prone areas at extreme hazard / BAL-40 or BAL-FZ

cross_references.cited: Planning Bulletin 67 Guidelines for Wind Farm Development (2004) (superseded)
State Planning Policy 1 State Planning Framework
Planning and Development (Local Planning Schemes) Regulations 2015
Environment Protection and Biodiversity Conservation Act 1999 (Cth)
Guidelines for Planning in Bushfire Prone Areas (DPLH/DFES 2017)
Visual Landscape Planning in WA manual (November 2007)
National Airports Safeguarding Framework Guideline D
EPA Guidance Statement No. 33 Environmental Guidance for Planning and Development (May 2008)
